# Compliance Checklist: Skill Generation Plugin Compliance

**Purpose**: Validate that requirements for skill generation compliance are complete, clear, consistent, and ready for implementation
**Created**: 2026-02-19
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 - Are description format requirements specified for ALL 5 code paths identified in research (optimizeCompileSkills, performSkillReorganization, optimizeSplitSkills, optimizeDemoteClaudeMD, PromoteEmbeddingToSkill)? [Completeness, Spec §FR-001/FR-010] — **Resolved**: FR-001 now explicitly lists all 5 code paths.
- [x] CHK002 - Is the behavior specified for when the LLM compiler produces a description that doesn't start with "Use when" despite being prompted to? [Completeness, Edge Case #2] — **Resolved**: Edge case #2 specifies blocking validation catches it; template fallback for description.
- [x] CHK003 - Is the behavior specified for when the skill theme contains special characters that break YAML frontmatter formatting? [Completeness, Edge Case #3] — **Resolved**: Edge case #3 specifies YAML-quoting for descriptions with special chars; slugify() handles slugs.
- [x] CHK004 - Are requirements defined for what happens to the existing `mem-*` skills in the DB `generated_skills` table during migration (slug column, name references)? [Completeness, Gap] — **Resolved**: FR-012 and Key Entities now document that DB stores slugs without prefix; naming change is filesystem/frontmatter only.
- [x] CHK005 - Is it specified whether `ExtractSkillDescription()` should be removed, deprecated, or retained for non-skill uses after replacement? [Completeness, Gap] — **Resolved**: Edge case added — deprecated and removed if no non-skill callers remain.
- [x] CHK006 - Are requirements defined for the LLM CompileSkill response format when LLM returns valid markdown but not valid JSON? [Completeness, Gap — relates to FR-006] — **Resolved**: Edge case on LLM empty/null description covers this — falls through to template.
- [x] CHK007 - Is the "sufficient information" threshold defined for FR-011 (what constitutes enough content to meaningfully populate all four sections)? [Completeness, Spec §FR-011] — **Resolved**: FR-011 now specifies coordination with existing `minClusterSize` (default 3 embeddings).

## Requirement Clarity

- [x] CHK008 - Is "third person" in FR-003 defined precisely enough to be machine-checkable (e.g., which pronouns are prohibited as subjects: "I", "you", "we", "my")? [Clarity, Spec §FR-003] — **Resolved**: FR-003 now lists prohibited pronouns and required style.
- [x] CHK009 - Is "content summaries or workflow descriptions" in FR-004 distinguishable from a well-written trigger description? Are examples of passing vs failing descriptions provided? [Clarity, Spec §FR-004] — **Resolved**: FR-004 now includes pass/fail examples.
- [x] CHK010 - Does US2 scenario 3 specify whether the system should "trim" OR "warn" for bodies exceeding 500 lines, or is either acceptable? [Clarity, Spec §US2-AS3] — **Resolved**: US2-AS3 now says "fails validation and is not written to disk" per blocking decision.
- [x] CHK011 - Is the `memory.` prefix format specified precisely — is it `memory.{slug}` with a dot separator, not `memory-{slug}` or `memory:{slug}`? [Clarity, Spec §FR-012] — **Resolved**: FR-012 explicitly says "dot separator" for frontmatter name.
- [x] CHK012 - Is FR-012's directory prefix `memory-` (hyphen) clearly distinguished from the frontmatter name prefix `memory.` (dot)? [Clarity, Spec §FR-012] — **Resolved**: FR-012 now says "dot separator" for name and "hyphen separator" for directory.
- [x] CHK013 - Is the 1024-character limit on descriptions measured in bytes or Unicode characters? [Clarity, Spec §FR-002] — **Resolved**: FR-002 now specifies "Go string length, i.e., UTF-8 byte count." Clarification session 2026-02-19 documents rationale.

## Requirement Consistency

- [x] CHK014 - Does SC-003's "description not appearing as a substring of the body" correctly express FR-010's intent? A legitimate trigger description containing a theme phrase could appear in the body without being "derived by truncating." [Consistency, Spec §SC-003 vs FR-010] — **Resolved**: SC-003 reworded to verify by confirming `generateTriggerDescription()` usage (template path) and JSON `description` field (LLM path), not by substring test.
- [x] CHK015 - Are FR-008's validation checks (description format, description length, body structure, body length) complete relative to all validation rules in data-model.md (V1-V8 includes naming and generated flag)? [Consistency, Spec §FR-008 vs data-model V7/V8] — **Resolved**: FR-008 now lists all 8 checks (V1-V8) including naming convention (V7) and generated flag (V8).
- [x] CHK016 - Does the forward-only clarification conflict with US1 scenario 3, which describes existing skills being updated "during a normal reorg cycle"? Is the reorg cycle itself considered "forward-only" or retroactive? [Consistency, Clarification #2 vs Spec §US1-AS3] — **Resolved**: Clarification session 2026-02-19 explains reorg cycles ARE forward-only — they apply new rules when skills are naturally regenerated, not retroactively.
- [x] CHK017 - Is the blocking validation behavior in FR-009 consistent with the existing `TestAndCompileSkill()` pattern, which also gates writes? Are both blocking steps coordinated or could they conflict? [Consistency, Spec §FR-009 vs research §R6] — **Resolved**: Clarification session 2026-02-19 explains sequential gates: compile → validate → write. No conflict.

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [x] CHK018 - Can SC-001 ("100% of generated skills") be measured automatically via a test, or does it require manual inspection of output files? [Measurability, Spec §SC-001] — **Resolved**: Clarification session 2026-02-19 confirms automatic measurement via unit tests.
- [x] CHK019 - Is SC-005 ("score 'pass' when evaluated against plugin skill criteria") defined with specific criteria, or does it depend on an external tool? [Measurability, Spec §SC-005] — **Resolved**: SC-005 now references all 8 specific V1-V8 validation checks and `ValidateSkillCompliance()` unit tests.
- [x] CHK020 - Does SC-004 ("blocks 100% of non-compliant skills") specify how the blocking count is surfaced (log output, return value, OptimizeResult field)? [Measurability, Spec §SC-004] — **Resolved**: SC-004 now specifies `OptimizeResult.SkillsBlocked` and `OptimizeResult.ValidationIssues`.

## Scenario Coverage

- [x] CHK021 - Are requirements specified for what happens when the LLM returns a valid JSON response but the `description` field within it is empty or null? [Coverage, Exception Flow] — **Resolved**: New edge case specifies LLM body kept, `generateTriggerDescription()` fallback for description.
- [x] CHK022 - Are requirements specified for the interaction between the migration (Task 1: `mem-` → `memory-`) and the dual guard (Task 2)? Specifically: during migration, old `mem:` prefixed skills temporarily exist — are they protected? [Coverage, Spec §FR-012 + FR-013] — **Resolved**: Migration runs first (renames all `mem-*` to `memory-*`). After migration, dual guard applies. Old `mem:` skills that haven't been migrated won't match `memory.` prefix guard, so they're protected from modification until migrated.
- [x] CHK023 - Are requirements defined for all generation paths that create skills from CLAUDE.md demotion (`optimizeDemoteClaudeMD` uses `description := theme`)? This path is distinct from the cluster-based paths. [Coverage, Spec §FR-001 applies to all paths] — **Resolved**: FR-001 now explicitly lists `optimizeDemoteClaudeMD` as one of the 5 covered paths.
- [x] CHK024 - Is there a requirement for what the optimize output should report when skills are skipped due to insufficient content (FR-011)? Is the skip count tracked? [Coverage, Gap] — **Resolved**: FR-014 added — `OptimizeResult.SkillsSkipped` tracks skip count. FR-011 also references this.
- [x] CHK025 - Are requirements specified for what happens when an existing `memory-{slug}/` directory already exists during migration from `mem-{slug}/`? [Coverage, Edge Case — migration collision] — **Resolved**: New edge case specifies migration skips and logs warning; existing `memory-{slug}/` content preserved.

## Edge Case Coverage

- [x] CHK026 - Is the minimum cluster size for skill generation defined? FR-011 says "sufficient information" but the existing code uses `minClusterSize` (default 3). Are these coordinated? [Edge Case, Spec §FR-011 vs existing code] — **Resolved**: FR-011 and Key Entities now specify coordination with `minClusterSize` (default 3).
- [x] CHK027 - Is behavior defined for when a skill's theme produces an empty or excessively long slug (e.g., theme is a single character, or 500 characters)? [Edge Case, Gap] — **Resolved**: Pre-existing behavior handled by `slugify()`. Theme is a required field; empty themes don't reach skill generation. Long themes are truncated by slugify. Out of scope for this feature.
- [x] CHK028 - Is behavior defined for when the `memory.` prefix + slug exceeds YAML line length limits or filesystem path length limits? [Edge Case, Gap] — **Resolved**: The `memory-` prefix adds 7 characters. OS path limits (255 filename, 4096 full path) make this extremely unlikely. Out of scope.
- [x] CHK029 - Is behavior defined for the case where a user creates a non-generated skill with a `memory.` prefix? The dual guard would match `isGeneratedSkill` even though it's user-created. [Edge Case, Spec §FR-013 — false positive risk] — **Resolved**: New edge case specifies `memory.` prefix is reserved. Without `generated: true`, dual guard protects. With both markers, user is opting into pipeline management.

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [x] CHK030 - Is the assumption documented that the DB `generated_skills.slug` column stores slugs WITHOUT the prefix, making the naming change purely a file-system and frontmatter concern? [Assumption, research §R7] — **Resolved**: FR-012 and new Key Entity document this explicitly.
- [x] CHK031 - Is it documented that `migrateMemoryGenSkills()` (existing migration) provides the pattern for the new `migrateMemSkills()` function? [Dependency, research §R7] — **Resolved**: Documented in plan.md Task 1 and research.md R7. No spec change needed (implementation detail).
- [x] CHK032 - Is the assumption documented that `SkillCompiler.CompileSkill()` signature remains unchanged and JSON parsing is handled by the caller? [Assumption, research §R5] — **Resolved**: Documented in contracts/skill-compliance.md. No spec change needed (implementation detail).

## Notes

- All 32 items resolved in Session 2026-02-19.
- CHK027, CHK028, CHK031, CHK032 resolved as out-of-scope or already documented in non-spec artifacts.
- No items required user input — all were resolvable from existing decisions, codebase patterns, and reasonable defaults.
